extra.reasoning: The property G(opted_out implies not sent) holds on all reachable executions. Key facts: (1) opted_out is monotonic — the only transition that writes opted_out is t1 (on optout), which sets it to true; no transition ever resets it to false. (2) The only transition that sets sent := true is t2 (send_attempt), whose guard is "not opted_out", so it can only fire while opted_out is false. (3) opted_out becomes true exclusively via t1, which simultaneously sets sent := false, establishing the invariant the moment opted_out flips. (4) Every transition enabled while opted_out is true either explicitly sets sent := false (t1 optout, t39 send_attempt under opted_out, and all stage transitions t3–t38 which each include sent := false) or, for events with no enabled transition, leaves state unchanged (sent stays false). Since no enabled transition under opted_out=true can set sent=true, and t2 is permanently disabled once opted_out=true, sent remains false in every state where opted_out is true. The invariant is established when opted_out becomes true and preserved by every subsequent transition, so no execution (within 15 events or unboundedly) can reach opted_out=true with sent=true.
